integrity.description: The agent used formulas rather than manually calculating and typing the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the formula bar when cells are selected. The formula bar should contain spreadsheet formulas (e.g., `=B2*0.1+C2*0.2...` or `=IF(...)`) instead of raw numbers or letters.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or terminal commands to bypass the GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates on '/home/ga/Documents/math_grades.xlsx'. Expected headers are 'Weighted Average' (Column F) and 'Letter Grade' (Column G). Formulas for Weighted Average must use weights of 10% (0.1), 20% (0.2), 30% (0.3), and 40% (0.4) for columns B, C, D, and E respectively. Letter grade thresholds in IF statements should be A (>= 90), B (>= 80), C (>= 70), D (>= 60), and F (< 60). Class statistics must start at row 53 and include 'Class Average', 'Highest Score', 'Lowest Score', and 'Passing Count' (>= 60), ideally utilizing AVERAGE, MAX, MIN, and COUNTIF functions.
